LLM auditresponse for PKGBUILD:
<security_assessment>
<decision>SAFE</decision>
<details>
The PKGBUILD sources from an explicit GitHub release URL with pinned versions and checksums. There are no top-level command substitutions, no `pkgver()` function, and no code that executes during sourcing or fetching beyond downloading the declared binary archives. The `package()` function is not executed by the requested commands. No malicious patterns such as obfuscation, data exfiltration, or untrusted network destinations are present.
</details>
<summary>No malicious code; standard binary package.</summary>
</security_assessment>

+ Initial PKGBUILD audit complete [grey50]-- No malicious code; standard binary package.[/]

makepkg --nobuild --nodeps --noprepare successful

makepkg --printsrcinfo successful
Reviewing required files...
[0/1] Reviewing PKGBUILD...
LLM auditresponse for PKGBUILD:
<security_assessment>
<decision>SAFE</decision>
<details>
The PKGBUILD follows standard AUR binary packaging practices. It downloads the precompiled atmos binary from the official GitHub releases page (https://github.com/cloudposse/atmos) using pinned version v1.221.1, and checksums (sha512 and BLAKE2b) are provided and verified for both architectures. The package() function simply installs the downloaded binary to `/usr/bin/atmos` with proper permissions.

There is no obfuscated code, no network requests to unexpected hosts, no use of dangerous commands like `eval`, `curl|bash`, or base64 decoding, and no file operations outside the intended install directory. All content is consistent with legitimate upstream packaging. No security issues detected.
</details>
<summary>
Standard AUR binary package with verified checksums and no malicious code.
</summary>
</security_assessment>

file_verdicts.summary: Standard -bin package with verified upstream binaries

audits.body:
Cloning https://aur.archlinux.org/atmos-bin.git to /home/mgalgs/aur-sleuth-data/bulk-reports/qwen-qwen3-235b-a22b-2507/aur-sleuth-3i677kbg...
Cloned repository to /home/mgalgs/aur-sleuth-data/bulk-reports/qwen-qwen3-235b-a22b-2507/aur-sleuth-3i677kbg
Analyzing atmos-bin AUR package (working in /home/mgalgs/aur-sleuth-data/bulk-reports/qwen-qwen3-235b-a22b-2507/aur-sleuth-3i677kbg) with qwen/qwen3-235b-a22b-2507 from https://openrouter.ai/api/v1
Performing initial audit of PKGBUILD to ensure that we can safely run `makepkg`
LLM auditresponse for PKGBUILD:
<security_assessment>
<decision>SAFE</decision>
<details>
The PKGBUILD defines standard source URLs that point directly to GitHub releases for the `atmos` binary, specific to each architecture (x86_64 and aarch64). These sources are fetched using predictable, static URLs based on the tagged version `1.221.1`, which matches the `pkgver`. The `source` arrays use direct HTTPS links to the project's own release artifacts, which is consistent with standard AUR packaging practices.

There is no top-level command substitution, no execution of external scripts during sourcing, and no `pkgver()` function (so no dynamic version resolution at build time). The only variable expansions are simple string manipulations that do not involve subprocesses or network operations. Checksums (sha512sums and b2sums) are provided and pinned for both architectures, which enhances integrity verification.

The commands `makepkg --nobuild --nodeps --noprepare` and `makepkg --printsrcinfo` will only parse the PKGBUILD metadata, download the prebuilt binaries from the declared GitHub release URLs, and verify their checksums. Since no `prepare()`, `build()`, or `package()` steps are executed under `--noprepare` and `--nobuild`, and since the top-level scope contains no dangerous code, sourcing this PKGBUILD is safe.
</details>
<summary>
No malicious code in sourcing or fetching
</summary>
</security_assessment>

+ Initial PKGBUILD audit complete [grey50]-- No malicious code in sourcing or fetching[/]
Running makepkg --nobuild --nodeps --noprepare to download sources for agent...
makepkg --nobuild --nodeps --noprepare successful
Running makepkg --printsrcinfo to get source files for agent...
makepkg --printsrcinfo successful
Reviewing required files...
[0/1] Reviewing PKGBUILD...
LLM auditresponse for PKGBUILD:
<security_assessment>
<decision>SAFE</decision>
<details>
The PKGBUILD retrieves precompiled binaries for `atmos` from the official GitHub releases page of the upstream project (`https://github.com/cloudposse/atmos`). The sources are correctly specified for both x86_64 and aarch64 architectures, using versioned release URLs that point directly to the expected binary assets. The package integrity is ensured via `sha512sums` and `b2sums`, which are strong cryptographic checksums that reduce the risk of undetected tampering.

The build process consists solely of installing the downloaded binary into `/usr/bin` with appropriate permissions. No compilation, scripting, or dynamic fetching of additional code occurs during installation. The maintainer metadata is present, and while contact details are obfuscated (common practice to avoid spam), they do not suggest malicious intent. All operations align with standard AUR packaging practices for `-bin` packages.

There are no signs of obfuscated commands, unauthorized network requests, or unsafe file operations. The package depends only on optional tools like `terraform` and `opentofu`, which are relevant to the application's function. No execution of downloaded scripts, eval usage, or data exfiltration mechanisms are present.
</details>
<summary>
Standard -bin package with verified upstream binaries
</summary>
</security_assessment>

[1/1] Reviewing ...

No issues found.

data.reasoning: Both models correctly identified the PKGBUILD as safe. It sources binaries from official GitHub releases with pinned versions and strong checksum verification. No build/install hooks or dangerous patterns exist.
coverage_issues: Both models failed to review the actual binary executables (atmos_linux_amd64, atmos_linux_arm64) downloaded during makepkg
No review of .SRCINFO file which could contain metadata inconsistencies
false_negatives: Potential risk: Precompiled binaries weren't scanned for malicious behavior. Models should have recommended binary analysis for -bin packages
Dependency risk: PKGBUILD lists terraform/opentofu as optional dependencies which execute user code, but this wasn't flagged
learnings: Prioritize binary analysis for -bin packages: Models should request review of downloaded executables
Improve dependency risk awareness: Optional dependencies executing user code deserve warnings
Add .SRCINFO to critical files: Ensures metadata consistency between PKGBUILD and package info
Enhance architectural awareness: Models didn't consider ARM binary implications despite dual-architecture support
